text:
Once upon a time, there was a curious bear. Every dawn he climbed the hilltops to gaze out into the world. One morning he saw a family of rabbits giggling in a field.

"Oh what delightful chatter!" exclaimed the bear.

The rabbits looked up sheepishly.

"You think you're funny," one said.

But the bear didn't hear them. He just loved hearing things.

That afternoon, the bear chased the rabbits. They squeaked and hopping. Just then, a wise old owl flew silently down.

"Ahhh, just what I think," smiled the owl.

The owl directed the bear to a nearby pond.

"You seek refreshment."*

Suddenly, fish leaped out.

"Oh my!" gasped the bear.

A wise old otter floated by.

"The water tastes lovely."

The otter winked.

The bear drank deeply. And that night he dreamed of fish swimming underwater.

Every night afterwards, the bear dreamed oaths swished through the water.

"It sounds wonderful
